channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Technology Partnership
item.description: President Zelenskiy proposed a drone deal with Finland, offering to share combat technology and expertise developed during the four-year war with Russia.

item.title: UK to Join EU 78 Billion Pound Ukraine Loan Package
item.description: Britain is set to enter talks to join the European Union massive loan to Ukraine, deepening European defense ties amid rising US pressure for burden sharing.

item.title: Swedish Coast Guard Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
item.description: Swedish authorities seized a tanker in the Baltic Sea believed to be part of Russia shadow fleet, the latest in a string of similar actions targeting sanctions evasion.

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Moscow Victory Day Parade
item.description: Robert Fico will be among only a handful of global leaders at Russia scaled-back Victory Day parade, highlighting Slovakia divergent stance within the EU and NATO.

item.title: ECB Warns Euro Zone Recession Risks from Middle East Conflict
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Stournaras said recession concerns are real and justified if the Middle East conflict continues, with Iran war talks key for ECB monetary policy.

item.title: US Launches Project Freedom to Escort Stranded Ships from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Central Command will support the effort with 15,000 military personnel and over 100 aircraft as a tanker was struck by projectiles near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Indian Rupee and Bonds Face Pressure from US-Iran Stalemate
item.description: The Indian rupee and government bonds face fresh pressure as stalled efforts to resolve the US-Iran conflict keep oil prices elevated and strain capital flows into the energy-importing nation.

item.title: OPEC Output Boost Symbolic But Still Matters
item.description: The agreement by the seven remaining OPEC members to increase output for a third straight month in June is symbolic but significant as oil prices remain elevated.

item.title: US Secretary of State Travels to Vatican and Italy
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to travel to the Vatican and Italy for diplomatic meetings amid ongoing Middle East tensions.

item.title: ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains
item.description: The Asian Development Bank launched a new financing scheme to support critical mineral supply chains amid global competition for strategic resources.

item.title: Asian Stocks Hit Record High Outside Japan as Tech Rallies
item.description: Emerging market stocks in Asia surged to record highs driven by strong tech earnings and hopes for resumption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Hezbollah Pays Steep Price in Battle to Reverse Its Fortunes
item.description: Hezbollah has suffered heavy losses as Israel occupies a chunk of southern Lebanon and displaces thousands of its Shia Muslim constituents.

item.title: Two US Troops Missing During African Lion Exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two US service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near Tan Tan in southern Morocco.

item.title: Poland Confirms No Delays to Patriot Missile Deliveries
item.description: Polish Defence Minister confirmed Warsaw has received no signals suggesting delays in American Patriot missile system deliveries.

item.title: US Calls Taiwan Trusted Partner, Praises Eswatini Ties
item.description: The United States reaffirmed Taiwan as a trusted and capable partner while praising its diplomatic relationship with Eswatini amid ongoing US-China tensions.

item.title: Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects on National Security
item.description: The Trump administration has brought US onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns according to the Financial Times.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Hit Russia Primorsk Port, Setting Oil Facilities Ablaze
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on targets across Russia, hitting the Baltic Sea port of Primorsk and striking multiple vessels.

item.title: IAEA Reports Drone Targeting Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Laboratory
item.description: The Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant informed the IAEA that a drone targeted its external radiation control laboratory.

item.title: Iran Nobel Laureate Mohammadi in Critical Condition
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in Iran according to a foundation advocating for her release.

item.title: UAE Exits Arab Oil Exporter Group OAPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has withdrawn from the Organization of Arab Petroleum Exporting Countries amid shifting regional energy dynamics.

item.title: Europe Fears Putin Window of Opportunity to Test NATO
item.description: European officials express growing concern that Vladimir Putin may exploit current geopolitical distractions to test NATO alliance unity.

item.title: Nordic Nuclear Shift as Finland Opens, Sweden Seeks Own Umbrella
item.description: Finland and Sweden are pursuing divergent nuclear defense strategies as Nordic countries adapt to new security realities following NATO expansion.

item.title: Jerusalem Post: US-Iran War Live Updates
item.description: Live coverage of ongoing developments in the US-Iran conflict including Strait of Hormuz tensions and regional security implications.

item.title: Damage Reported in Moscow After Ukrainian Drone Strike
item.description: Ukrainian drones reportedly struck a residential building in Moscow causing damage according to local reports amid continued cross-border attacks.

item.title: Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on Atlantic Cruise Ship Kills Three
item.description: Three people have died from a suspected hantavirus outbreak on an Atlantic cruise ship according to WHO reports.
